english: Singapore fresh spring roll premium x2
Popiah with chicken cutlet and prawns x2

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'spring roll' class (FOODON_03311069). Popiah is a Southeast Asian fresh (non-fried) spring roll variant with a soft wheat wrapper; no dedicated FoodOn class exists for popiah specifically. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers spring rolls generically. No dedicated code for Singaporean popiah or premium popiah with chicken and prawns. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for popiah or Singaporean fresh spring roll. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular Southeast Asian dish codes.

preparation_method: fillings may be stir-fried or blanched

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Popiah (Premium) x2 consists of two rolls, each approximately 140 g, giving a total serving weight of approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: Popiah (fresh spring roll, mixed filling) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists standard popiah (fresh spring roll with mixed vegetable and protein filling) per 100 g. The premium variant (GD000983) adds chicken cutlet, prawns, and crabstick on top of the standard filling, increasing protein and fat slightly. Per-100g values are adapted from HPB Foodo standard popiah baseline with upward adjustment for the premium protein additions, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central ingredient data for chicken breast and shrimp to estimate the incremental macro contribution. The two-roll serving (approximately 280 g) is consistent with Qi Ji restaurant portion sizing.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; fresh spring roll / popiah analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Singaporean popiah (premium) is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 52 is estimated from the closest analogous food: fresh spring roll / Vietnamese rice-paper roll with mixed protein and vegetable filling, which has a reported GI of approximately 50–55 (low category) in the Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables. The wheat-skin popiah wrapper has a moderate starch content but the high vegetable and protein load suppresses the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 51.8) / 100 ≈ 27.0 g (serving carbohydrate = 18.5 g/100g × 280 g = 51.8 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg/100 g; ~1092 mg per 2-roll serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 2-roll serving provides approximately 1092 mg sodium, representing nearly 47% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg, primarily from hoisin sauce, chilli sauce, and seasoned fillings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~27 per 2-roll serving) with low GI (52) but substantial carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that while the GI is low (52), the 2-roll serving delivers approximately 52 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of 27, which requires careful portion management and should be paired with non-starchy vegetables or consumed as a meal rather than a snack to avoid postprandial glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from chicken cutlet and crabstick
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content (~1092 mg per serving) is the primary concern and warrants portion restriction; saturated fat is moderate (~3.1 g per serving) and the inclusion of prawns contributes dietary cholesterol (~154 mg per serving), so consumption should be occasional and accompanied by a low-sodium dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Popiah (Premium) x2 is a Singaporean fresh spring roll dish served as two rolls by Qi Ji restaurant. Popiah is a traditional Hokkien-Teochew fresh spring roll made with a thin, soft wheat flour skin (popiah skin) wrapped around a savoury filling. The premium version elevates the classic recipe by adding chicken cutlet, whole prawns, and crabstick to the standard filling of shredded jicama (bangkuang), carrot, bean sprouts, and lettuce, all bound with hoisin sauce and chilli sauce and garnished with fried shallots. Unlike fried spring rolls, popiah is served fresh and unfried, giving it a soft, moist texture with a contrast of crunchy vegetables and tender proteins. Each roll weighs approximately 140 g, and the two-roll serving totals approximately 280 g. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate energy density (~148 kcal/100 g), meaningful protein (~8.2 g/100 g) from the chicken, prawns, and crabstick, and a useful dietary fibre contribution (~2.1 g/100 g) from the vegetable filling. The primary clinical concern is sodium (~390 mg/100 g; ~1092 mg per serving), which is high due to the sauces used. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), making it a relatively moderate glycemic food, though the total carbohydrate load per serving is substantial. Popiah is culturally significant in Singapore and is commonly eaten as a snack, light meal, or festive food during Chinese New Year. It is not halal-certified in this form due to the mixed protein sources and sauce ingredients. The dish is dairy-free and contains allergens including gluten, crustaceans, fish, egg, and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 2 popiah rolls?
Popiah vs fried spring roll calories

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for Qi Ji Premium Popiah x2. Per-100g values are adapted from HPB Foodo standard popiah baseline with upward protein and fat adjustment for the premium additions (chicken cutlet, prawns, crabstick), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central ingredient data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for fresh spring rolls. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base food (standard popiah) is well-documented in HPB Foodo and the premium additions are nutritionally well-characterised in USDA data.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo popiah entry or the USDA ingredient cross-reference data used for this record. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data for this dish. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo popiah entry. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; this is a confirmed zero as the dish uses no hydrogenated fats and HPB Foodo reports negligible trans fat for standard popiah. 5. The serving size of 280 g (2 × 140 g rolls) is estimated from typical Qi Ji popiah portion weights; exact restaurant weight was not independently verified. 6. Halal status is listed as false because the dish contains prawns and crabstick alongside chicken, and the field dataing does not carry a MUIS halal certification in the field survey source data. 7. GI is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for this specific premium popiah product has not been published. 8. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and several micronutrients are estimated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient-level data and should be treated as approximate.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from HPB Foodo baseline adapted for premium popiah. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Populated ontology with honest closest_parent codes. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Added GI analogous estimate with full documentation.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for q8. Identified that 'Bao Bing (Gao Ji) x2' was present in aliases_by_language.romanized but missing from the flat aliases list. Added 'Bao Bing (Gao Ji) x2' to the flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Identified root cause of persistent q8 failure. The aliases_by_language.hindi array contained 'पोपिया (प्रीमium) x2' — a mixed-script string with Latin characters 'ium' embedded in the Devanagari word for 'premium' — which did not match the correct full-Devanagari string 'पोपिया (प्रीमियम) x2' in the flat aliases list. Corrected the hindi alias to use the identical full-Devanagari string 'पोपिया (प्रीमियम) x2' in both aliases_by_language.hindi and the flat aliases array. Performed full cross-check of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases list; all 18 strings now match verbatim.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
